title: Measuring the acceptability of EQ-5D-3L health states for different ages: a new adaptive survey methodology
journal: The European Journal of Health Economics
abstract: The acceptability set of EQ-5D-3L quantifies societal preferences concerning age and disease severity. Its measurement profiles and potential role in health resource allocation needs further exploration.
summary_en: Health-resource allocation benefits from knowing societal preferences over different health states at different ages. The EQ-5D-3L "acceptability set" quantifies these preferences. This paper presents a new adaptive-survey methodology to measure the set more efficiently across demographic groups. The approach allows more precise estimation at lower respondent burden. A methodological contribution useful for health-technology assessment and resource-allocation decisions in healthcare systems.
data_used: Survey data using an adaptive-survey methodology to measure health-state acceptability across age groups.
policy_relevance: For Hungarian health-technology assessment (GYEMSZI, NEAK) and for EU-level health-economic-evaluation methodology. The paper provides a concrete tool for more accurate quality-adjusted life-year (QALY) measurement in Hungarian cost-effectiveness analyses. Relevant to drug-reimbursement decisions and to healthcare priority-setting. Caveat: methodological; operational deployment requires survey-design investment.
